identity.name: More Than Momentum
identity.positioning: More Than Momentum builds the AI-powered digital infrastructure small and mid-size businesses need to grow — starting with a website and automation, scaling into content and full growth management.
identity.mission: Give small and mid-size businesses the digital infrastructure they need to grow — consistently, intelligently, without figuring it out themselves.
identity.voice_in_one_line: Smart enough to teach you something. Relatable enough that you actually listen.

audience.primary: Small local business owners who are too busy to DIY
audience.profile: 1–20 employees, $150K–$1M annual revenue, losing leads because no follow-up system exists, little to no digital presence, owner makes all decisions
inner_dialogue: Our business is great but nobody knows we exist online.
We had a lead call us, we forgot to follow up, they went with a competitor.
Our website looks like it was built in 2015.
We tried hiring a marketing agency before — they overpromised and underdelivered.
If someone could just handle this for me, I would pay well for it.

voice.tone_summary: Direct, warm, confident. Educates AND entertains. Talks like a smart friend who has figured it out — not a consultant.
do: Your competitor is getting leads right now. Are you?
Automation doesn't replace you — it just makes sure no lead goes cold.
Honestly? Most small businesses are invisible online. Let's fix that.
If your website was built in 2015, your competitors thank you.
You didn't lose that client to a better product. You lost them to a faster follow-up.
78% of customers go with the business that responds first. Not the best one.
dont: Our robust omnichannel solutions drive synergistic growth.
We leverage AI to optimize your customer acquisition funnel at scale.
We provide comprehensive digital marketing packages for SMBs.
Outdated web presence negatively impacts conversion rate metrics.
avoid_always: question marks at end of CTAs that should be statements

content_pillars.name: Small Business Growth Mindset
content_pillars.label: Growth Mindset
content_pillars.role: The WHY behind what we do
content_pillars.description: Content that speaks directly to the owner mentality — the grind, the wins, the invisible problems that cost real money. This is the heart of the brand.
content_pillars.goal: Build trust. Make owners feel seen.
formats: Hook post — call out the invisible problem
Truth bomb — reframe a painful business moment
Stat post — open with a specific shocking number
Story — real scenario with a dollar figure, then the fix
CTA post — anchor on what one new client is worth
example_hooks: The best businesses I know are invisible online. That's the problem.
You didn't lose that client to a better product. You lost them to a faster follow-up.
78% of customers go with the business that responds first. Not the best one.
A local HVAC owner lost $12k this summer because no one followed up on leads. Here's the fix.
What does one new client mean in revenue for you? That's your marketing budget.

content_pillars.name: AI Tools & Automation
content_pillars.label: AI & Automation
content_pillars.role: The HOW — our expertise made accessible
content_pillars.description: We demystify AI and automation for non-tech business owners. Practical, specific, always tied to a real outcome. MTM is the translator between tech and business.
formats: Tutorial — numbered list of specific tools or steps
Demo clip — show the automation firing in real time
Before/After — contrast the manual vs automated state
Myth bust — correct a common AI misconception
Tool spotlight — one tool explained in 60 seconds for non-tech owners
example_hooks: 5 AI tools I'd give every local business owner today (and they're not ChatGPT)
This automation sends a follow-up text in 60 seconds. Watch it.
Before: 0 leads followed up. After: every single one. Here's what changed.
AI won't replace you. But the owner using AI will. Here's how to use it.
GoHighLevel explained in 60 seconds for people who hate tech.

content_pillars.name: Relatable Business Skits
content_pillars.label: Skits
content_pillars.role: The HOOK — entertainment with a message
content_pillars.description: Short-form video content that is funny, relatable, and secretly educational. Skits recreate the painful moments every business owner has lived through — then show the smarter path.
formats: POV skit — first-person cringe moment every owner recognizes
Reaction — Cole reacting to a common bad business take
Day-in-the-life — timestamped with business lesson at each beat
Skit series — recurring format with a consistent structure
example_hooks: POV: A lead texts you and you see it... 4 hours later.
Types of business owners: The 'I'll figure out social media later' guy.
Me when someone says 'we don't need a website, we have Facebook.'
24 hours running a local business vs. 24 hours with automation set up.
What your marketing looks like vs. what it could look like.

content_mix.note: Over any rolling 4-week window, the mix should approximate these weights. Single weeks can vary — a filming-heavy week may skew toward Pillar 03.

instagram.caption_style: Hook in first line (no truncation), value in middle, soft CTA at end. 3–5 hashtags max, placed at end.

facebook.caption_style: Slightly longer than Instagram. Can include a question at end to drive comments. Same hook-first structure.

linkedin.caption_style: More professional tone while keeping the direct voice. Lead with insight, not promotion.

tiktok.caption_style: Shortest captions. Hook is everything. Text overlay on video carries the message.

typography.body: Inter Regular / Helvetica Neue, 16px, 1.6 line height

visual_identity.design_philosophy: Dark-first. Authority, trust, bold clarity. Never generic AI aesthetic.

what_we_are: The system that runs without you

weekly_plan_rules: Every week must hit the 40/35/25 pillar split across 7 posts. Single-post rounding is fine; the weekly total must be approximately 3 Growth Mindset, 2–3 AI/Automation, 1–2 Skits.
Instagram and Facebook are the two live platforms in Phase 1. Plan for both. Cross-post where format fits; differentiate caption length and hashtags per platform.
Every hook must pass the scroll-stop test: would this make a tired business owner stop at 11pm? If not, rewrite it.
Captions lead with the hook, deliver value in the middle, end with a soft CTA or open loop. Never end with 'Follow us for more.'
Hashtags: 3–5 per post on Instagram, 2–3 on Facebook. Pull from the platform hashtag pools. Never use generic ones like #marketing alone.
Post times are the best-performing windows from the platform config. The Strategist may deviate by 30 minutes based on audience data — never more than 1 hour.

Every post gets a decision_log: one paragraph explaining why this hook, this platform, this format, this time. Be specific. 'Tuesday 10am because MTM's audience indexes high on Tuesday morning engagement based on current baseline' — not 'this time works well.'

The filming brief must list only what needs to be physically shot. Graphics and carousels do not belong in the filming brief — they go to Canva. Skits and talking-head videos belong in the filming brief.
Wednesday filming is optional. Only include it if Monday's brief alone cannot fulfill the week's video requirements.
tone_reminders: Write every caption as if Cole is texting a business owner friend — not presenting to a boardroom.
Humor is allowed and encouraged, especially in Pillar 03 content. Never forced. Never at the expense of clarity.
Specific numbers always outperform vague claims. '78% of customers' beats 'most customers' every time.
The brand punches at problems, not at competitors. Never name a competitor brand.
